div.head: Memorandum of Conversation, by the Under Secretary of State (Welles)
div.p: The British Chargé d’Affaires, Sir Ronald Campbell, called to see me this morning at his request.

Sir Ronald stated that about a week ago, by instruction of his Government, he had communicated to Secretary Hull a suggestion from the British Government that the United States transmit to Finland assurances from the Soviet Government that the latter, if peace were now made between Finland and Russia, would respect the frontier of Finland. He stated that Secretary Hull had assured him that he would let him know what the decision of this Government might be but that he had not as yet any word and that his Government considered the matter urgent.

I told the Minister that I had had no opportunity of discussing this question with Secretary Hull but that I would do so and inform Sir Ronald accordingly. I stated that it seemed to me that the mere acting by the United States Government as a transmitting agency would give the impression that this Government was offering at least a tacit guarantee but that nothing short of an actual guarantee by the United States and Great Britain would be considered satisfactory by the Finnish Government in view of their experience of the last year. I said that I assumed that this Government was not in a position under present conditions to offer any such guarantee as would be satisfactory to Finland.
